description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the tmaier/docker-compose:latest Docker image with a docker:dind service and define variables APP_DIR, APP_IMAGE, E2E_DIR, E2E_IMAGE, DOCKER_DRIVER. Define two stages, build and deploy. In the build stage add a build job that logs in to registry.gitlab.com with gitlab-ci-token, pulls $E2E_IMAGE:latest and $APP_IMAGE:latest (ignoring failures), builds both images using --cache-from and tags them with $CI_COMMIT_SHA and latest, then runs docker-compose -f docker-compose.ci.yml up --build -d, executes docker-compose exec -T app npm run test-all, docker-compose exec -T app npm run build, and docker-compose exec -T e2e npm run test-all, and finally pushes $E2E_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA, $E2E_IMAGE:latest, $APP_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA and $APP_IMAGE:latest. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs only on the master branch, builds the production stack with docker-compose -f docker-compose.production.yml build, runs docker-compose -f docker-compose.production.yml run --rm app npx webpack --config webpack.config.production.js, copies app/public to a public directory, and publishes the public directory as a GitLab Pages artifact. No cache configuration is used.
